---
title: "Germany Used Cars 2023"
subtitle: "End-to-End Machine Learning Analysis"
author: "Anja Kadic"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: white
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    transition-speed: fast
    footer: "Germany Used Cars 2023 · ML Analysis · Anja Kadic"
    logo: ""
    code-block-height: 400px
    fig-align: center
    margin: 0.08
    css: slides_custom.css
    preview-links: false
---

## Project Overview {background-color="#faf9f6"}

**Problem:** Used-car pricing is opaque — buyers and sellers struggle to know if a listing is fairly priced.

**Solution:** Train ML models on **240,000 real AutoScout24 listings** to answer two questions:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Classification**

Is this car in the **HIGH** or **LOW** price segment?

Threshold: **€19,486** (training median only)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Regression**

What is the expected listing price **in euros**?

Target: raw `price_in_euro`
:::
:::

---

## Dataset at a Glance {background-color="#faf9f6"}

**Source:** AutoScout24 Germany 2023 — scraped listings from Kaggle

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Raw rows | 251,079 |
| After cleaning | **240,017** (95.6% retained) |
| Training set | 192,013 rows (80%) |
| Test set | 48,004 rows (20%) |
| Features (raw) | 8 |
| Features after encoding | **559** |

4 categorical + 4 numeric → one-hot encoded to 559 model inputs

---

## Data Cleaning — 12 Sequential Steps {background-color="#faf9f6"}

All filters applied **one at a time** — each step operates on already-filtered rows:

| Step | Rule | Removed |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Drop rows missing price / power / mileage | ~390 |
| 2 | Drop corrupted fuel_type entries | 88 |
| 3 | Drop `transmission_type == "Unknown"` | 1,134 |
| 4 | Year ≥ 2000 (no classic cars) | 1,924 |
| 5 | Price ≥ €500 (no scrap/data errors) | 77 |
| 6 | Price ≤ €80,000 (no ultra-luxury) | 7,078 |
| 7–8 | Mileage 1–500,000 km | ~460 |
| 9–10 | Power 40–800 PS | 64 |
| 11–12 | Drop 5 redundant/low-quality columns | — |

**Result:** 251,079 → **240,017 rows** · No target leakage

---

## EDA — Price Distribution

![](../reports/figures/price_clean.png){width=80%}

After cleaning: concentrated €500–€80,000 · median **€19,488** · skewness ≈ 1.2

---

## EDA — Key Relationships {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../reports/figures/top_brands.png)

Volkswagen dominates (32k listings) followed by Mercedes-Benz, Audi, Opel, BMW
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../reports/figures/price_vs_mileage.png)

Clear negative relationship — higher mileage → lower price (strong predictor)
:::
:::

---

## Feature Engineering {background-color="#faf9f6"}

Raw `year` was replaced by two engineered features:

**`car_age = 2023 − year`**

- Depreciation depends on age, not calendar year
- Makes the linear term in Ridge/Logistic interpretable
- Avoids perfect collinearity (year and car_age cannot both be included)

**`mileage_per_year = mileage_in_km / max(car_age, 1)`**

- Captures *usage intensity*, not just total usage
- 200,000 km over 20 years = normal · over 5 years = heavy use
- `max(car_age, 1)` prevents division by zero for 2023 listings

---

## Leakage-Safe Pipeline Architecture {background-color="#faf9f6"}

```
ColumnTransformer
├── OneHotEncoder (brand, fuel_type, transmission_type)
├── OneHotEncoder(min_freq=50) ← model column (1,191 unique values)
└── StandardScaler (car_age, power_ps, mileage_in_km, mileage_per_year)
↓
VarianceThreshold(0.0) ← removes constant features (0 removed)
↓
XGBoostClassifier / XGBoostRegressor
```

**Leakage prevention:**

- `price_in_euro` excluded from classification features entirely
- Segment threshold (€19,486) from **training median only**
- Encoders and scaler fitted on `X_train` only — never see test set
- Test set used exclusively for **final reported metrics**

---

## Classification — Model Ladder

| Model | F1 (macro) | ROC-AUC | Train F1 | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Dummy (most_frequent) | 0.333 | 0.500 | 0.333 | 0.000 |
| Logistic Regression | 0.929 | 0.978 | 0.933 | 0.004 |
| Decision Tree (depth=15) | 0.919 | 0.945 | 0.949 | **0.030** |
| **XGBoost (tuned)** | **0.934** | **0.982** | 0.946 | 0.012 |

- **F1_macro** is honest: Dummy F1_binary = 0.667 but F1_macro = **0.333**
- Logistic Regression nearly matches XGBoost → price segment is nearly **linearly separable**
- Decision Tree has the worst overfitting (gap = 0.030)

---

## Classification — Evaluation Plots {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../reports/figures/roc_curve.png)

XGBoost AUC = **0.982** · Logistic close behind at 0.978
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../reports/figures/confusion_matrix.png)

XGBoost on 48,004 held-out cars · very few misclassifications
:::
:::

---

## Regression — Model Ladder

| Model | R² | MAE (€) | RMSE (€) | Train R² | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Dummy (mean) | 0.000 | 10,915 | 14,369 | 0.000 | 0.000 |
| Ridge (α=0.1) | 0.841 | 3,761 | 5,726 | 0.841 | **0.000** |
| Decision Tree (depth=15) | 0.856 | 3,279 | 5,452 | 0.911 | 0.055 |
| **XGBoost (tuned)** | **0.905** | **2,602** | **4,430** | 0.922 | 0.017 |

- Ridge zero train-test gap reveals **strong additive pricing structure**
- XGBoost adds +0.064 R² from non-linear brand × age × mileage interactions
- Mean error of **€2,602** on unseen listings using only 7 inputs

---

## Regression — Evaluation Plots {.smaller}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../reports/figures/regression_predicted_vs_actual.png)

Predicted vs actual · tight diagonal · some spread at high prices
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../reports/figures/feature_importance_reg.png)

Car age and brand are the dominant pricing factors
:::
:::

---

## What the Results Reveal {background-color="#faf9f6"}

**Used-car pricing is fundamentally additive**

- Ridge R² = 0.841 with **zero overfitting** — linear model captures most variance
- XGBoost's edge (+0.064 R²) comes from interaction effects, not from the additive base

**Dominant pricing factors (from feature importance):**

1. **Brand** — prestige premium is large and non-linear
2. **Car age / mileage** — depreciation curves differ by brand
3. **Horsepower** — strong positive correlation
4. **Model** — within-brand variation (Golf vs Tiguan)

**XGBoost captures what Ridge cannot:**
mileage matters *more* for cheap cars than expensive ones; age penalty differs by brand

---

## Live Deployed Application

**[https://germany-used-cars-ml.streamlit.app/](https://germany-used-cars-ml.streamlit.app/)**

Three interactive features beyond basic prediction:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
**Why this price?**

Rule-based explanation comparing mileage, age, power, and brand against the dataset — with honest "likely" language
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
**Similar cars**

Top-8 real listings nearest to your inputs — filtered by model → brand → numeric distance
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
**What if comparison**

Configure two cars side by side, predict both, and see the price difference in € and %
:::
:::

---

## Limitations {background-color="#faf9f6"}

**In scope:**

- General-market used cars, Germany, 2023
- Year 2000–2023 · Price €500–€80,000 · Power 40–800 PS

**Out of scope — model may give unreliable predictions for:**

| Category | Why |
|---|---|
| Pre-2000 / oldtimers | Age **increases** value (collector logic) |
| New and dealer-demo cars | MSRP pricing, not depreciation |
| Ultra-luxury (>€80,000) | Brand prestige and rarity dominate |
| Non-German markets | Different taxation, preferences, supply |
| Future prices | Reflects 2023 market; EV transition ongoing |

**Note:** Model predicts **asking price**, not negotiated sale price (typically 5–15% lower)

---

## Conclusion {background-color="#faf9f6"}

**Final model performance on 48,004 held-out test listings:**

| Task | Model | Metric | vs Baseline |
|---|---|---|---|
| Classification | XGBoost | F1_macro **0.934** | +0.601 vs dummy (0.333) |
| Regression | XGBoost | R² **0.905** · MAE **€2,602** | RMSE ÷ 3.2 vs dummy |

**Key takeaways:**

- Price segment is nearly **linearly separable** — additive features dominate
- XGBoost wins from interaction effects (brand × age × mileage), not complexity alone
- **€2,602 mean error** on unseen cars using 7 inputs demonstrates practical utility
- Full leakage-safe pipeline ensures reported metrics reflect real-world performance

**Code & data:** [github.com/anjakadic178-tech/germany-used-cars-ml](https://github.com/anjakadic178-tech/germany-used-cars-ml)
